text:
In the quiet town of Harmonia, nestled between whispering woods and gentle streams, lived a peculiar bear who was loved by all for his infectious laughter and gentle nature. Bear Marvin was also known for his peculiar sense of time—never quite keeping track of the day or the hour, his calendar was a swirling mass of sticky notes and scraps of paper.

One day, the town held a picnic in the town square, inviting everyone to partake in a spread of homemade food. The sky had promised a sunny day, and children played football in the outfield, while elders sat on wooden benches at the picnic tables, sharing stories and laughter.

As Marvin approached with a large bowl of strawberry shortcake, he paused, feeling uncertain. "Um, this is peach shortcake for me," he said, scratching his fuzzy chin thoughtfully. The townsfolk exchanged puzzled glances, but with friendly smiles, they began assembling Marvin’s special plate.

Halfway through his meal, Marvin realized something was off.
